title: ranunculus cornutus var. cornutus
intro: Ranunculus cornutus var. cornutus, also known as the horned buttercup, is a flowering plant species within the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly as an emetic and purgative, to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders. The plant is also believed to have health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's rhizomes and roots are typically dried and used as a tincture or decoction, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
